<svg height="58" width="229"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">PAT Outside Interface
(2 interface)</text></svg>

<svg height="58" width="225"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">PAT Inside Interfaces
(2 interfaces)</text></svg>

<svg height="233" width="393"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">Distribution Layer configure DIS1 & 2:

Layer 3 interfaces
OSPF 1 area 0 to core
VLANS:
o   VLAN 10 – 10.0.10.0/24 
o   VLAN 20 – 10.0.20.0/24 
o   VALN 30 – 10.0.30.0/24 
o   VLAN 40 – 10.0.40.0/24 </text></svg>

<svg height="183" width="374"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">Access Layer 1,2,3,4 configure:

Create VLAN
Access ports for the VLAN
Trunk Ports to the distribution layer 
Enable RPVST
Port security</text></svg>

<svg height="158" width="395"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">Core Layer CORE1 & 2 configure:

Layer 3 interfaces
etherchannel (PAGP)
port channel 1
OSPF 1 area 0 to Edge and distribution</text></svg>

<svg height="233" width="359"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">EDGE1 & 2 configure:

Layer 3 interfaces
OSPF 1 area 0 to core
PAT (inside and outside interfaces)
Default route to core
iBGP between EDGE
eBGP to ISP neighbors
</text></svg>

<svg height="133" width="521"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">ISP1 & 2 configure:

Layer 3 interfaces (EDGE and ISP are Dual homed) 
Default route to EDGE
eBGP to EDGE neighbors</text></svg>

<svg height="133" width="484"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">There are multiple ways to connect the ISP and
EDGE. 
Can use default routes for simplicity. 
Or BGP
</text></svg>
<svg height="33" width="62"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">PAGP</text></svg>

<svg height="258" width="452"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">SVIs 
DHCP pools for VLANS 10,20,30,40
Enable RPVST
Trunk ports to access and distribution
Tune active HSRP with spanning tree
DIS1
HSRP primary 10,20 secondary 30,40
DIS2
HSRP primary 30,40 secondary 10,20
Summerize (LANs) to the CORE uisng EIGRP</text></svg>

<svg height="108" width="286"><text fill="#000000" fill-opacity="1.0" font-family="MS Shell Dlg 2" font-size="18.0" font-weight="bold">End hosts:

Obtain IP address via DHCP
Dynamic or Static</text></svg>
